feed.title: Thys
feed.subtitle: My journey with Deneb, a Power BI custom visual

entry.title: The one with the sparklines.
entry.summary:
This chart was part of the Week 14 Workout Wednesday challenge.
  Dataset The dataset can be found on the Workout Wednesday link above.
  Fields used
Sub-Category Monthend_Date Sales Profit
  Specification
The specification in a nutshell:
facet * row spec * layer * text mark * text mark * area mark

entry.title: The one with the boxplot
entry.summary:
This chart was a Tableau Workout Wednesday challenge. Week 12 Tableau Workout Wednesday
  Dataset The dataset is part of the presentation on the Workout Wednesday challenge above.
  Additional fields created
Jitter Jitter = RANDBETWEEN(2,18)
  Fields used
Playoffs Team Name Age Jitter

entry.title: The one with the timeline
entry.summary:
This chart was part of my submission for the Week 13 Workout Wednesday challenge. I did not think it necessary to add a post for this, but I was requested to add it - so here goes.
  Dataset The dataset is part of the presentation on the Workout Wednesday challenge. The data was entered into a table using Power Query enter data. The table had two columns, the description and ...

entry.title: The one with the bullet chart.

link.title: The one with the bullet chart.

entry.summary:
This chart was part of Week 13 Workout Wednesday challenge. For more information on the bullet chart, please see the Data Visualisation Catalogue for more information on the usage of the chart type.
  Dataset The dataset can be found on the Workout Wednesday link above.
  Additional fields created
Only one additional field created in Power Query. The MaxValue...

entry.title: Getting Started with Deneb
entry.summary:
What is Deneb?
As quoted on Deneb’s site, Deneb is … a certified custom visual for Microsoft Power BI….
It’s developed by Daniel Marsh-Patrick. The custom visual is build on Vega and Vega-lite visual language.
Please visit the Deneb website. Here you will find more information on how to get started with this custom visual.
Why do I use Deneb?
Deneb is a lot of fun to work with. I love to ...
